docs.title: Synup SDK
docs.text: Python SDK for building custom applications on top of Synup's infrastructure.

docs.title: What is this?
docs.text: The Synup SDK is a pure API client — a thin, developer-friendly wrapper around Synup's v4 REST API. It's built for teams who want to build their own dashboards, automations, or integrations on Synup's backend.

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text: pip install synup-sdk

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from synup import SynupClient

client = SynupClient(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")

# Fetch locations
result = client.fetch_all_locations(first=10)
for loc in result["locations"]:
    print(loc["name"], loc["city"])

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text: Component Description SDK (pip install synup-sdk) Pure API client — 97 methods covering all Synup endpoints Examples 10 runnable scripts for common use cases Dashboard Streamlit app to explore and test endpoints interactively API Reference Auto-generated docs for every method

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text:
• Thin wrapper — returns raw API data, no opinionated models
  • Predictable naming — fetch_* for GET, create_*/update_* for POST
  • Minimal required params — sensible defaults, optional pagination
  • Auto-encoding — numeric location IDs are base64-encoded automatically

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Auto-generated from SDK source code docstrings.

docs.title: synup.client.SynupClient
docs.text:
Client for the Synup v4 API. Create with an API key; all methods return structured data.

Example

client = SynupClient(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY") locations = client.fetch_all_locations(first=10) if locations.get("success"): for loc in locations["locations"]: print(loc["name"])

docs.title: fetch_all_locations(first=None, after=None, before=None, last=None, fetch_all=False, page_size=100)
docs.text:
Get locations for the account, with optional pagination or fetch-all.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default first int | None

How many locations to return from the start of the list.

None after str | None

Pagination cursor — return locations after this cursor.

None before str | None

Pagination cursor — return locations before this cursor.

None last int | None

How many locations to return from the end of the list.

None fetch_all bool

If True, automatically follow pages and return one flat list of all locations.

False page_size int

When using fetch_all, how many locations to request per API call (default 100).

100

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is False: dict with success, locations (list), page_info (has_next_page, end_cursor, etc.).

list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is True: list of location dicts (id, name, city, storeId, etc.).

Example

result = client.fetch_all_locations(first=10) if result.get("success"): for loc in result["locations"]: print(loc["name"])

docs.title: fetch_locations_by_ids(location_ids)
docs.text:
Get locations by a list of IDs. Accepts numeric or base64-encoded IDs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str | int]

List of location IDs. Use numbers (e.g. 16808) or base64 strings; both are accepted and normalized automatically.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of location dicts (id, name, street, city, etc.). Empty list if none found.

Example

locations = client.fetch_locations_by_ids([16808, 16749])

docs.title: fetch_locations_by_store_codes(store_codes)
docs.text:
Get locations that match the given store codes (e.g. STORE01, STORE02).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default store_codes list[str]

List of store identifiers as used in your account.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of location dicts. Empty list if none match.

Example

locations = client.fetch_locations_by_store_codes(["STORE01", "STORE02"])

docs.title: search_locations(query, fields=None, first=None, after=None, before=None, last=None, fetch_all=False, page_size=100)
docs.text:
Search locations by keyword (name, address, or store ID).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default query str

Search term to match against location name, street, or store_id.

required fields list[str] | None

Optional. Restrict search to specific fields, e.g. ["name"] or ["store_id"]; if omitted, all are searched.

None first, after, before, last

Pagination; same semantics as fetch_all_locations.

required fetch_all bool

If True, return one flat list of all matching locations.

False page_size int

When fetch_all is True, how many to fetch per request.

100

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is False: dict with success, locations, page_info.

list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is True: list of location dicts.

Example

result = client.search_locations("cafe", first=20) locations = result["locations"]

docs.title: fetch_locations_by_folder(folder_id=None, folder_name=None)
docs.text:
Get all locations in a folder (including subfolders). Use folder_id or folder_name.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default folder_id str | None

Folder UUID from your account.

None folder_name str | None

Human-readable folder name (e.g. "franchise"). Either this or folder_id is required.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of location dicts. Empty list if folder is empty or not found.

Example

locations = client.fetch_locations_by_folder(folder_name="franchise")

docs.title: fetch_locations_by_tags(tags, archived=None, first=None, after=None, before=None, last=None, fetch_all=False, page_size=100)
docs.text:
Get locations that have any of the given tags. Optional filter by archived status.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default tags list[str]

List of tag names (e.g. ["recent", "vip"]). Locations with any of these tags are returned.

required archived bool | None

If True, only archived locations; if False, only active; if None, both.

None first, after, before, last

Pagination cursors and limits.

required fetch_all bool

If True, return one flat list of all matching locations.

False page_size int

When fetch_all is True, page size per request.

100

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is False: dict with success, locations, page_info.

list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is True: list of location dicts.

Example

locations = client.fetch_locations_by_tags(["recent"], archived=False, fetch_all=True)

docs.title: fetch_interactions(location_id, first=None, after=None, before=None, last=None, site_urls=None, start_date=None, end_date=None, category=None, rating_filters=None, fetch_all=False, page_size=100)
docs.text:
Get reviews and social interactions for a location (default: last 30 days).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64-encoded.

required first, after, before, last

Cursor-based pagination.

required site_urls list[str] | None

Optional. Restrict to specific sites (e.g. maps.google.com, yelp.com).

None start_date, end_date

Optional. Date range in YYYY-MM-DD.

required category str | None

Optional. "Review" or "Social".

None rating_filters list[int] | None

Optional. Star ratings to include (e.g. [4, 5]).

None fetch_all bool

If True, return one flat list of all interactions.

False page_size int

When fetch_all is True, items per request.

100

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is False: dict with success, interactions, page_info, total_count.

list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any]

If fetch_all is True: list of interaction dicts.

Example

result = client.fetch_interactions(16808, first=10, rating_filters=[4, 5]) reviews = result["interactions"]

docs.title: create_location(input)
docs.text:
Create a new location in your account. Use camelCase keys (name, storeId, street, city, etc.).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input dict[str, Any]

Location data. Required: name, storeId, street, city, stateIso, postalCode, countryIso, phone. Optional: description, ownerEmail, ownerName, and others per API.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with location (created object), success (bool), and errors (if any). Branch on success.

Example

result = client.create_location({"name": "Acme Inc", "storeId": "ACME01", "street": "123 Main St", "city": "NYC", "stateIso": "NY", "postalCode": "10001", "countryIso": "US", "phone": "5551234567"})

docs.title: update_location(input)
docs.text:
Update a location. Pass id (location ID) plus any fields to change (e.g. phone, name).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input dict[str, Any]

Must include id (base64 or numeric). Other keys are fields to update (camelCase).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with updated location, success, and optional errors.

Example

client.update_location({"id": "TG9jYXRpb246MTM2OTc=", "phone": "5559876543"})

docs.title: archive_locations(location_ids)
docs.text:
Archive one or more locations (they are hidden, not deleted). Accepts numeric or base64 location IDs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str]

List of location IDs (numeric or base64-encoded).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with success and any errors. Check success to confirm.

Example

client.archive_locations(["TG9jYXRpb246MTM5OTg="])

docs.text:

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str]

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with success and any errors.

Example

docs.title: cancel_archive_locations(location_ids, selection_type, changed_by)
docs.text:
Cancel scheduled archival for the given locations.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str]

Base64-encoded location IDs.

required selection_type str

"ALL_ITEMS" or "SELECTED_ITEMS".

required changed_by str

Identifier of who is performing the action (e.g. user email or id).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with success and any errors.

Example

client.cancel_archive_locations(["TG9jYXRpb246ODQ3NzM="], "SELECTED_ITEMS", "admin@example.com")

docs.title: add_location_photos(location_id, photos)
docs.text:
Add one or more photos to a location. Each item needs photo (URL) and type (LOGO, COVER, or ADDITIONAL).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required photos list[dict[str, Any]]

List of dicts with "photo" (URL string) and "type" (LOGO, COVER, or ADDITIONAL). Optional: partnerMediaId.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with addLocationPhotos result; check success for outcome.

Example

client.add_location_photos(16808, [{"photo": "https://example.com/logo.png", "type": "LOGO"}])

docs.title: remove_location_photos(location_id, photo_ids)
docs.text:
Remove photos from a location. Only ADDITIONAL photos can be removed; pass their photo IDs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required photo_ids list[str]

List of base64 photo IDs to remove.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with removeLocationPhotos result.

Example

client.remove_location_photos(16808, ["TG9jYXRpb25QaG90bzoxMjI2MA=="])

docs.title: star_location_photos(location_id, media_ids, starred)
docs.text:
Mark location photos as starred or unstarred. Account can have at most 4 starred photos.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required media_ids list[str]

List of media IDs to star or unstar.

required starred bool

True to star, False to unstar.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with starUnstarLocationPhotos result.

Example

client.star_location_photos(16808, ["TWVkaWFGaWxlOjg4MjY5Nw=="], starred=True)

required

docs.title: create_review_campaign(location_id, name, location_customers, screening=None, landing_page_template=None, opening_email_template=None, sms_template=None, email_details=None, sms_details=None)
docs.text:
Create a review campaign for a location and optionally add customers and templates.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required name str

Campaign name.

required location_customers list[dict[str, Any]]

List of dicts with name; optional email and phone (e.g. [{"name": "John", "email": "j@example.com"}]).

required screening, landing_page_template, opening_email_template, sms_template

Optional campaign config.

required email_details, sms_details

Optional dicts for email/SMS (e.g. subject, content).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with reviewCampaign, success, and errors. Branch on success.

Example

result = client.create_review_campaign(16808, "Holiday Feedback", [{"name": "John", "email": "j@example.com"}], screening=False)

docs.title: add_review_campaign_customers(review_campaign_id, location_customers)
docs.text:
Add customers to an existing review campaign. Pass campaign ID and list of customer dicts.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default review_campaign_id str

UUID of the review campaign.

required location_customers list[dict[str, Any]]

List of dicts with at least name; optional email, phone.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with add result and success.

Example

client.add_review_campaign_customers("794be682-a321-4eac-953c-37dcac0a55a2", [{"name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com"}])

docs.title: add_keywords(location_id, keywords)
docs.text:
Add keywords to a location for ranking tracking. Returns the created keyword objects.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required keywords list[str]

List of keyword strings (e.g. ["plumber", "plumbing near me"]).

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of created keyword dicts (id, name, etc.).

Example

created = client.add_keywords(16808, ["plumber", "plumbing near me"])

docs.title: archive_keyword(keyword_id)
docs.text:
Archive a keyword so it is no longer tracked. Pass the keyword's base64 ID.

Base64-encoded keyword ID (from fetch_keywords or add_keywords).

required

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with archived keyword and success.

client.archive_keyword("S2V5d29yZDo3NjQzMTE=")

docs.title: fetch_ranking_analytics_timeline(location_ids, from_date, to_date, source)
docs.text:
Get ranking analytics timeline (positions over time) for locations and a source (e.g. Google).

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str]

List of base64-encoded location IDs.

Date range in YYYY-MM-DD.

List of source names (e.g. ["Google"]).

required

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of timeline/rollup dicts by date.

Example

timeline = client.fetch_ranking_analytics_timeline(["TG9jYXRpb246NzkwODQ="], "2023-03-11", "2023-03-15", ["Google"])

docs.title: fetch_ranking_sitewise_histogram(location_ids, from_date, to_date, source)
docs.text:
Get ranking histogram by keyword count for locations and a source (e.g. Google).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_ids list[str]

List of base64-encoded location IDs.

required from_date, to_date

Date range in YYYY-MM-DD.

List of source names (e.g. ["Google"]).

required

List of histogram/rollup dicts.

docs.title: fetch_users()
docs.text:
Get all users in the account.

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

List of user dicts (id, email, firstName, lastName, role, etc.).

docs.title: create_user(email, role_id, first_name, last_name=None, direct_customer=None, **extra)
docs.text:
Create a user in the account with the given role. Pass email, role ID, and first name.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default email str

User's email address.

required role_id str

Base64 role ID (from your account's roles).

required first_name str

User's first name.

required last_name str | None

Optional. User's last name.

None direct_customer bool | None

Optional. Whether user is a direct customer.

None **extra Any

Optional. Additional fields passed to the API.

{}

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with user, success, and errors. Branch on success.

Example

result = client.create_user("user@example.com", "Q3VzdG9tUm9sZToyMDgzMQ==", "Jane", last_name="Doe")

docs.title: update_user(user_id, email=None, role_id=None, first_name=None, last_name=None, phone=None, archived=None, direct_customer=None, **extra)
docs.text:
Update a user. Pass user ID and any fields to change (email, role, name, phone, etc.).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default user_id str

Base64 user ID to update.

required email, role_id, first_name, last_name, phone, archived, direct_customer

Optional. Only include what you want to change.

required **extra Any

Optional. Additional fields for the API.

{}

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with user, success, and errors.

Example

client.update_user("VXNlcjo5OTY0", first_name="Jane Updated")

docs.title: add_user_locations(user_id, location_ids)

docs.title: create_temporary_close_automation(name, start_date, start_time, end_date, location_id)
docs.text:
Create an automation that temporarily closes a location and reopens on a set date (Google/Facebook).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Name for the automation.

required start_date str

Close date in YYYY-MM-DD.

required start_time str

Close time (e.g. "10:00:00").

required end_date str

Reopen date in YYYY-MM-DD.

required location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with flow, success. Branch on success.

Example

result = client.create_temporary_close_automation("Holiday closure", "2025-02-25", "10:00:00", "2025-02-28", 85006)

docs.title: get_oauth_connect_url(location_id, site, success_url, error_url)
docs.text:
Get a URL to connect a Google or Facebook profile to a location. Link is valid 24 hours. Redirect the user to the returned URL.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required site str

"GOOGLE" or "FACEBOOK".

required success_url str

Where to redirect after successful connection (your app URL).

required error_url str

Where to redirect after error (your app URL).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with url (redirect the user here). Check for presence of url.

Example

result = client.get_oauth_connect_url(16808, "GOOGLE", "https://yoursite.com/success", "https://yoursite.com/error") redirect_user_to(result["url"])

docs.title: oauth_disconnect(location_id, site)
docs.text:
Disconnect a Google or Facebook profile from a location. Use for a single location.

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required site str

"GOOGLE" or "FACEBOOK".

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with disconnect result and success.

Example

client.oauth_disconnect(16808, "FACEBOOK")

docs.title: connect_google_account(success_url, error_url)

Get a URL to connect a Google account for bulk use across locations. Valid 24 hours. Redirect the user to the returned URL.

Your app URL for successful connection redirect.

required error_url str

Your app URL for error redirect.

required

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with url. Redirect user to url to start connection.

Example

result = client.connect_google_account("https://ok.com", "https://err.com")

required

required

docs.title: create_grid_report(location_id, keywords, business_name, business_street, business_city, business_state, business_country, latitude, longitude, distance, distance_unit, grid_size)
docs.text:
Create a Local Rank Grid report (ranking positions across a geographic grid). Max 25 keywords.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default location_id str | int

Location ID — numeric or base64.

required keywords list[str]

List of keywords (max 25).

required business_name, business_street, business_city, business_state, business_country

Business address fields.

required latitude, longitude

Center of the grid (decimal degrees).

required distance int

Grid radius (number).

required distance_unit str

"mi" or "km".

required grid_size int

3, 5, or 7 (grid dimension).

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with data.reportIds and errors. Branch on presence of errors.

Example

result = client.create_grid_report(16808, ["italian restaurant"], "Chianti", "No 12, 5th Block", "Bengaluru", "Karnataka", "India", 12.935216, 77.619961, 20, "km", 3)

docs.title: synup.exceptions.SynupAPIError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised when the API returns a non-2xx response. Use status_code and response_body to handle or display.

Attributes:

Name Type Description status_code

HTTP status (e.g. 401, 404, 500). Use to branch: 401 → check API key; 404 → resource missing; 5xx → retry or contact support.

response_body

Raw response text from the API; often JSON with an error message or validation details.

Example

try: client.fetch_all_locations() except SynupAPIError as e: if e.status_code == 401: print("Invalid or missing API key. Check Settings → Integrations.") else: print(f"Request failed: {e.status_code} — {e.response_body}")

docs.title: SDK Explorer Dashboard
docs.text: An interactive Streamlit app to browse and test all SDK endpoints — no code required.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
cd dashboard
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Run
docs.text:
SYNUP_API_KEY=your_api_key streamlit run app.py

Opens at http://localhost:8501.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Browse by category — Locations, Listings, Reviews, Analytics, Users, etc.
  • Search — Find any endpoint by name
  • Auto-generated forms — Input fields are built from method signatures
  • Execute & inspect — Call any endpoint and see the JSON response
  • Error details — Full error messages with HTTP status and response body

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text:
After launching, you'll see:

  1. Sidebar — API key input, category filter, search
  2. Main area — Select an endpoint, fill in parameters, hit Execute
  3. Response — JSON result displayed below

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install synup-sdk

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Get your API key from Settings > Integrations > Generate in your Synup workspace.

from synup import SynupClient

client = SynupClient(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")

Or use an environment variable:

import os
from synup import SynupClient

client = SynupClient(api_key=os.environ["SYNUP_API_KEY"])

docs.title: Your First API Call
docs.text:
# Fetch the first 5 locations
result = client.fetch_all_locations(first=5)

if result.get("success"):
    for loc in result["locations"]:
        print(f"{loc['name']} — {loc['city']}, {loc['stateIso']}")

    # Check if there are more pages
    if result["page_info"]["has_next_page"]:
        cursor = result["page_info"]["end_cursor"]
        next_page = client.fetch_all_locations(first=5, after=cursor)

docs.title: Fetch All (Auto-Pagination)
docs.text:
For bulk operations, use fetch_all=True to automatically follow all pages:

all_locations = client.fetch_all_locations(fetch_all=True)
print(f"Total: {len(all_locations)} locations")

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All API errors raise SynupAPIError with the HTTP status code and response body:

from synup import SynupClient, SynupAPIError

try:
    client.fetch_all_locations()
except SynupAPIError as e:
    if e.status_code == 401:
        print("Invalid API key")
    else:
        print(f"Error {e.status_code}: {e.response_body}")

docs.title: Location IDs
docs.text:
Most methods accept location IDs as either:

  • Numeric: 16808
  • Base64-encoded: "TG9jYXRpb246MTY4MDg="

The SDK auto-encodes numeric IDs, so both work:

# These are equivalent
client.fetch_premium_listings(16808)
client.fetch_premium_listings("TG9jYXRpb246MTY4MDg=")

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Browse Use Cases for runnable examples
  • Open the Dashboard to test endpoints interactively
  • See the full API Reference

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
Runnable example scripts in the examples/ folder. Set your API key first:

export SYNUP_API_KEY="your_api_key"

docs.title: 1. Quick Start — Fetch Locations
docs.text:
from synup import SynupClient
import os

client = SynupClient(api_key=os.environ["SYNUP_API_KEY"])
result = client.fetch_all_locations(first=5)
for loc in result["locations"]:
    print(f"{loc['name']} — {loc['city']}")

Run: python examples/01_quickstart.py

docs.title: 2. Bulk Export to CSV
docs.text:
Export all locations to a CSV file with auto-pagination.

import csv
locations = client.fetch_all_locations(fetch_all=True)
with open("locations.csv", "w", newline="") as f:
    writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=["name", "city", "phone"], extrasaction="ignore")
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(locations)

Run: python examples/02_bulk_export_locations.py

docs.title: 3. Review Monitoring
docs.text:
Scan locations for negative reviews that need attention.

reviews = client.fetch_interactions(location_id, first=20)
for review in reviews["interactions"]:
    if review.get("rating", 5) <= 2:
        print(f"NEEDS ATTENTION: {review['authorName']} ({review['rating']} stars)")

Run: python examples/03_review_monitoring.py

docs.title: 4. Bulk Respond to Reviews
docs.text:
Auto-reply to unanswered reviews using templates (or LLM-generated responses).

client.respond_to_review(
    interaction_id="review-uuid",
    response_content="Thank you for your feedback!"
)

Run: python examples/04_bulk_respond_reviews.py

docs.title: 5. Analytics Report
docs.text:
Pull Google, Bing, and review analytics across all locations.

google = client.fetch_google_analytics(location_id, from_date="2024-01-01", to_date="2024-12-31")
review_stats = client.fetch_review_analytics_overview(location_id, start_date="2024-01-01", end_date="2024-12-31")

Run: python examples/05_analytics_report.py

docs.title: 6. Listings Audit
docs.text:
Audit listing sync status across locations — flag unsynced listings.

# Premium listings (Google, Yelp, Facebook, etc.)
premium = client.fetch_premium_listings(location_id)
for l in premium:
    if l.get("syncStatus") != "SYNCED":
        print(f"Not synced: {l['site']} — {l['syncStatus']}")

# Voice assistant listings
voice = client.fetch_voice_listings(location_id)

# AI listing suggestions
ai = client.fetch_ai_listings(location_id)

Run: python examples/06_listings_audit.py

docs.title: 7. User Management
docs.text:
Create users, assign roles, and control location access.

result = client.create_user("user@example.com", role_id, "Jane", last_name="Doe")
client.add_user_locations(user_id, [loc_id_1, loc_id_2])

Run: python examples/07_user_management.py

docs.title: 8. Google Connect Flow
docs.text:
Full OAuth flow for connecting Google Business Profiles.

# Get OAuth URL
result = client.connect_google_account("https://app.com/success", "https://app.com/error")
# After connection, list accounts and trigger matching
accounts = client.fetch_connected_accounts(publisher="google")

Run: python examples/08_google_connect_flow.py

docs.title: 9. Grid Rank Reports
docs.text:
Create Local Rank Grid reports to see your Google rankings across a geographic area.

result = client.create_grid_report(
    location_id=16808,
    keywords=["pizza near me"],
    business_name="Joe's Pizza",
    business_street="123 Main St", business_city="NYC",
    business_state="NY", business_country="US",
    latitude=40.7128, longitude=-74.006,
    distance=10, distance_unit="mi", grid_size=3,
)

Run: python examples/09_grid_rank_report.py

docs.title: 10. Review Campaigns
docs.text:
Create campaigns to solicit reviews from customers.

result = client.create_review_campaign(
    location_id=16808,
    name="Q1 Feedback",
    location_customers=[{"name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}],
)

Run: python examples/10_review_campaign.py
